RE: 
Conflict Of Interest Laws Applicable To 
Emeryville City Council Members 
Thank you for sending us a copy of the July 14th letter to you from 
James White, City Attorney for Emeryville. 
The purpose of this letter is to 
present additional background information to you which we believe to be relevant 
in considering Mr. White's request, and to present to you our point of view. 
It should be understood that this proposed conversion is not a "routine" 
conversion. 
In size, it is the largest conversion even attempted in California 
and, as far as we know, it is the largest such attempt ever in the United States. 
Watergate represents a unique situation in America. 
With 2000 residents, we 
represent more than 50% of the adult population of Emeryville, Of 2,408 registered 
voters in Emeryville. 53% or 1,267 live at Watergate. 
The 2,000 residents have 
opted for a life in an alternative complex having no children and, if the con-
version is completed, there will be no comparable housing available nearby for 
those who cannot afford what appear to be very high prices for units constructed 
subject to minimum building codes on filled land. 
So much for background. 
With respect to the conflict of interest described 
in Mr. White's letter, it is based on the fact that the three City Council Members 
who live at Watergate will be "permitted to purchase their unit for approximately 
$4,000 less than the purchase price offered to the general public." In actuality, 
the discount will be 6% of the purchase price; a copy of the July 13th letter to 
residents from Pacific Union Company will be mailed under separate cover de-
scribing this situation. 
We will also send you, as information, a copy of the 
June 15th letter from Pacific Union representing the first communication to 
tenants from the new owners. 
We do not believe that a conflict of interest exists for the three Council 
Members who live at Watergate simply because they are not, to our knowledge, being 
offered any different financial discount than is being offered to the other 2,000 
residents at Watergate. 
We believe that the potential conflict can be easily 
solved by having the Council Members involved disclose to the City the estimated 
financial impact involved in their individual situations. 
The problem is further

June 15, 1978 
Dear Watergate Resident: 
On June 1, 1978 we became the new owners of the Watergate Apartments .• 
Our group is composed of the Pacific Union Company of San Francisco 
and The Watergate Company. 
We are proud of the Watergate apartment 
complex and look forward to the challenge of maintaining and improving 
the pleasant quality of life in this community . 
. 
Our first objective is to manage Watergate as the best apartment complex 
possible. 
We plan to go slowly and make as few changes as possible until we are 
familiar with all aspects of the complex's operation and thus our current 
rental policy for existing residents will remain the same. 
All leases 
will be respected, of course, as a lease is a valid contract regardless 
of who owns the complex. 
Residents whose leases expire will not be 
required to re-lease and will automatically go on a month to month tenancy 
with no increase in rent. 
The policy of adding electric metering to units 
whose leases expire will continue as before. 
We have received many inquiries regarding the possible conversion of 
Watergate to condominiums. 
We have no specific information that will be 
helpful to you at this time. 
However, we will be contacting each resident 
in the near future to discuss the possibilities and answer your questions 
as best we can. 
Should you have any questions, please come to the office and we will be 
glad to help in any way we can. 
We look forward to a long and pleasant 
association with you and with Watergate. 
Si ncere ly,

Dear Watergate Resident: 
July 13 > 1978 
We would like to give you specific information regarding our conversion pl~ns 
here at Watergate. 
It is our hope that this complex will continue to provide you with a unique home 
lifestyle, close to the geographical center of the San Francisco Bay Area. 
Our 
plan is to provide each of you the increased advantage of having the opportunity 
to own the unit in which you reside. This will be done by converting the apartment 
complex from one ownership to condominiums, where 1,249 individuals own their own 
unit and share the ownership of the common areas such as land, streets, Clipper 
Club, etc., through a homeowner's association. 
While we are conducting this process, the Company has agreed for the present not 
to increase the rents to current residents and to offer six mo~~renewals on 
expired leases. 
We plan to keep the present operational staff and only make administrative changes 
where it is deemed an improvement. 
There are tremendous advantages to you in the conversion process and we hope those 
of you who plan on staying here recognize the benefits. 
In addition to being in 
a position to purchase your home at a favorable price, several improvements will 
occur here at Watergate. 
Watergate Company intends as part of its plans, to correct the deficiencies that 
have been identified in the project, with special emphasis on security, water 
leaks and common area rehabilitation. 
It has been shown that people will have 
a greater commitment because of their decision to own here and the population will 
become less transient. 
The City of Emeryville will share in the benefits of a 
stable population. 
We are aware that there are those of you who are not in a financial position to 
consider buying or who will elect not to buy but would like to stay on as renters. 
In this regard, you should understand that we expect the phasing program to be

At last night's "work session" of Emeryville City Planning Commission, you indicated 
that you would be receiving communications from other governmental agencies about 
the "conflict of interest" question concerning Gary Kalian and Hs. Patricia Jones 
in the August 23rd public hearing about the proposed Watergate condominium con-
version. 
We would like to receive copies of whatever transpires on this matter on 
a timely basis. 
As you probably know, our position is that Gary, being an employee of F. P. Lathrop 
Construction Company, should probably be excluded from participation in the hearing. 
However, we see no conflict with respect to Hs. Jones. 
Acknowledging that she rents 
an apartment at Watergate, as far as we know, she is being offered exactly the same 
terms as offered to the other 2,000 renters at Watergate. 
We see no conflict, and 
we disagree with your opinion, dated January 31, 1978 or July 31, 1978, that she 
should not be able to vote on this matter. 
We think there is no reason that she 
should be excluded from voting. 
We would like to see you amend your opinion in 
this regard. 
With respect to our request above to receive timely notice of developments, we hope 
that in good faith you will agree to this. 
A few weeks ago, as you have acknowledged 
to me publicly and privately, you withheld two public documents from the Tenants 
Association upon advisement or someone stlll unnamed -
I hope thaL yuu will be 
able to be more cooperative now.

Dear Mr. Margason: 
August 17, 1978 
I'm sure that you are probably aware of the planned conversion of Watergate 
Apartments to condominiums. 
At the August 16th "work session" of the Emery-
ville City Planning Commission, Jose Hurtado, City Engineer, indicated that 
a predecessor firm to yours rendered an opinion on the soil conditions prior 
to initial approval of the Watergate construction by City Council. 
At 
yesterday's meeting, Mr. Hurtado indicated that, his opinion was that your 
initial report, occurring around 1969 or earlier, was still valid in 1978, 
and that there would be no need for an update at this time. 
We have three questions to you at this time: 
1. 
Can we have a copy of your findings in 1969 or earlier? 
We will be glad to pay for any copying charges involved. 
2. 
Would your firm, in 1978, be willing to state that whatever 
conclusions you arrived at in 1969, or before, are unchanged 
now, based upon conditions existing now or would you need to 
make another study now, nine years later? It has been implied 
that no further studies are necessary now. 
3. 
If your answer is that no further studies are needed now, 
would you be willing to render an opinion to Emeryville 
City Council to that effect, with a copy to this Association? 
Continued

Dear Sirs: 
The Watergate Tenants Association represents about 2,000 residents comprlslng

We are now faced wi th a possibility that the 1,249

Emeryville City Attorney in this matter.

requesting an opinion as to "whether or not any Conflict of 
Interest laws of the State would be violated by Council members 
of the City" on certain facts. 
However, this letter did not really 
give the FPPC all the relevant facts for that Commission to make an 
intelligent judgment, and appears to beg for a conflict ruling. 
We 
sent a Mailgram to FPPC when we finally determined the facts of his 
inquiry and the FPPC ruled that there was no conflict. 
The problem 
here was, in our opinion, Mr. I-lhite' s supplying only part of the 
picture to the Commission.

July 23rd l'failgram are

mission Chairman, Mr. Gary Kalian,

ATTENTION:Gary Kalian, Chairperson 
You have requested that I render to you a legal opinion as 
to whether any of the members of the Planning Commission of 
the City of Emeryville may be in a position of Conflict of 
Interest with respect to the Application of the Watergate 
Company for approval of a Subdivision Map which has been 
filed with the City and which will soon be before the Planning 
Commission for a hearing and decision. 
Also, if any member 
of the Commission is found to be in a position of Conflict of 
Interest, 
you have requested that I render a further opinion 
as to the effect of such Conflict of Interest upon such mem-
ber with respect to hearing and deciding the Watergate appli-
cation. 
Ordinance No. 475 of the City of Emeryville created the City 
Planning Cor.~ission. 
Section 29.105 of said Ordinance provides 
that any member who has an interest in the subject matter being 
considered by the Commission shall disclose such interest and 
shall be disqualified from voting thereon. 
That Section also 
defines the types of interest which would result in a Commissioner 
disqualification. 
The types of interest are either: 
(1) an ownership or leasehold interest in any 
property involved in the matter being con-
sidered by the Commission; 
(2) employment by the owner or holder of a lease-
hold interest in such property; 
(3) a relationship by blood or marriage to the owner 
or holder of a 
leasehol~ interest in such property. 
Based upon the above provisions of Ordinance 475, it is my legal 
opinion that any member of the Planning Commission who is either 
a tenant of the Watergate Apartments, or who owns an interest in

As the City Attorney for the City of Emeryville, I 
am requesting an opinion from your Commission as to whet-
her or not any Conflict of Interest laws of the State 
would be violated by Councilmembers of the City on the 
following facts: 
Three members of the City Council are tenants residing 
in the Watergate Apartments which is located in the City 
of Emeryville. 
The owner of these apartments has applied 
to the City of Emeryville for approve of a subdivision 
map for the purpose of converting the apartments to condo-
miniums. 
At some point in the process the City Council will 
be required to give its approval of the subdivision map 
based upon findings and recommendations of the Planning 
Commission. 
If the subdivision obtains the City's approval 
and is converted to condominiums, tenants in the Watergate 
Apartments will be permitted to purchase their unit for 
approximately $4,000 less than purchase price offered to 
general public. 
The Tenants Association is proposing that the City adopt 
an ordinance which would prevent the conversion of the 
Watergate Apartments to condominiums if less than 35% of 
the tenants vote in favor of the conversion. 
In order for 
the ordinance to be enacted, at least one of these three 
aforementioned Councilmembers would be required to vote in 
favor of it.

Interest laws if anyone of these three Councilmembers 
approved the subdivision map?

Conflict of Interest laws if anyone of these Councilmembers 
voted either to approve or deny approval of the ordinance? 
Because a vote on at least one of these issues may be 
required Goon, I am requesting an early response to this 
letter of inquiry. 
Thank you for your early consideration 
of this matter. 
Very truly yours, 
James White

Lowenstein, Chairman RE: Conflict of Interest Laws Applicable to Emeryville City Council Members Dear Sirs: This letter is a confirmation of our July 23rd Mailgram to you about the above matter. In this regard, we enclose copies of the following: 1. Our July 23rd letter sent to you as a Mailgram. 2. The June 15th letter from Pacific Union Company, representing their initial communication to the tenants and proposing a month-to-month tenancy when leases expire. 3. Our June 29th letter to Pacific Union Company. 4. Their July 13th letter to tenants outlining some tentative prices, etc. 5. Our July 21st letter to the Chairman of the Planning Commission calling for consideration of our proposed ordinance before final approval of the proposal is granted. We would like your timely consideration of the questions posed in our July 23rd letter. Lowenstein, Chairman Dear Sirs: RE: Conflict Of Interest Laws Applicable To Emeryville City Council Members Thank you for sending us a copy of the July 14th letter to you from James White, City Attorney for Emeryville. The purpose of this letter is to present additional background information to you which we believe to be relevant in considering Mr. White's request, and to present to you our point of view. It should be understood that this proposed conversion is not a "routine" conversion. In size, it is the largest conversion even attempted in California and, as far as we know, it is the largest such attempt ever in the United States. Watergate represents a unique situation in America. With 2000 residents, we represent more than 50% of the adult population of Emeryville, Of 2,408 registered voters in Emeryville. 53% or 1,267 live at Watergate. The 2,000 residents have opted for a life in an alternative complex having no children and, if the con- version is completed, there will be no comparable housing available nearby for those who cannot afford what appear to be very high prices for units constructed subject to minimum building codes on filled land. So much for background. embedding.summary: The Watergate Tenants Association requests FPPC guidance on whether three Emeryville City Council members residing at Watergate Apartments have a conflict of interest in voting on a condominium conversion proposal that would offer residents a 6% discount on purchases. The Association argues no conflict exists since all residents receive the same discount and seeks confirmation that Council members should be permitted to vote on the matter.
